description_long: This object is a solar energy infrastructure installation featuring multiple rectangular solar panels mounted on a flat, low-profile structure that resembles a building rooftop. The panels are dark, likely composed of photovoltaic cells, and are arranged in two distinct clusters, each set at a noticeable tilt to maximize sunlight exposure. The supporting structure beneath the panels appears to be a solid, light-colored base, possibly concrete or reinforced material, providing stability and elevation. The solar panels are framed, likely with metal, and are elevated above the base, suggesting a roof-mounted system. No visible cabling or inverter components are apparent, and the installation is expansive, covering a significant surface area. The overall design is utilitarian, optimized for efficient solar energy capture in an urban environment.
description: A large, roof-mounted solar panel installation with two tilted clusters of dark photovoltaic panels on a light, flat base. The structure is expansive and designed for efficient solar energy collection.
description_view_0: Front view showing two main clusters of solar panels, with the larger cluster in the foreground and the smaller one behind, both mounted on a flat base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the pronounced tilt of the solar panels and the elongated, low-profile base structure beneath them.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back edges of the solar panels and the depth of the supporting base, with less detail on the panel surfaces.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the angular arrangement of the two panel clusters and the full length of the base.
attribute_panel_orientation: Panels are tilted at approximately 30 degrees, orientation appears fixed, azimuth direction not clearly discernible.
attribute_mounting_structure_type: roof-mounted on a flat base, likely with an aluminum or steel frame structure.
attribute_array_configuration: Two clusters of panels, each in a rectangular grid arrangement; approximately 8 panels in the larger cluster and 4 in the smaller cluster.
